skill.name: Skywork Design
skill.description: Generate or edit images via backend Skywork Image API. Use for any image creation, poster design, logo design, visual asset generation, or image modification request. Supports text-to-image and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio and resolution control.
skill.summary: Generate and edit professional images via Skywork AI API with support for text-to-image, image editing, and multiple aspect ratios and resolutions.
skill.author: SkyworkAI

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 263 potential issues, all evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate image generation tool using Skywork AI API. External command flags stem from markdown code fences. Crypto flags are base64 encoding (not encryption). Network/filesystem access is standard API integration. OAuth browser flow uses standard subprocess calls. No malicious behavior detected.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution - Legitimate OAuth Flow
high_findings.description: subprocess.Popen used to open default browser for OAuth login. Commands are standard system utilities (open/xdg-open/start) with hardcoded Skywork login URLs.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matches subprocess.Popen but context is legitimate OAuth authentication with hardcoded URLs. No user input in command construction.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Scanner flags base64 encoding as 'weak crypto'. Base64 is NOT a cryptographic algorithm - it provides no encryption or security. This is a static analyzer misidentification.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: base64 is encoding, not encryption. Used for transmitting image data as ASCII. No security claims made or broken.

medium_findings.title: External Command Detection in Markdown - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flags backtick characters in markdown files as 'shell backtick execution'. These are standard markdown code fences (```) for documentation formatting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation files use code fences for formatting. No shell execution occurs in these files.

low_findings.title: Network Access to Skywork API - Expected Behavior
low_findings.description: HTTP requests to api.skywork.ai and related endpoints. This is expected API integration for image generation service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate API endpoints hardcoded. No data exfiltration or unauthorized connections.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access for Token Storage - Expected Behavior
low_findings.description: Read/write access to ~/.skywork_token for authentication persistence. Standard token caching pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard OAuth token caching. Token stored in user home directory with proper file permissions.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access - Expected Configuration
low_findings.description: Access to SKYBOT_TOKEN, SKYWORK_API_BASE, SKYWORK_GATEWAY_URL. All are legitimate configuration variables for the Skywork service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All environment variables are SKYWORK-prefixed and used for legitimate service configuration.

content.value_statement: Creating custom images for branding, marketing, and design projects requires specialized tools and significant time. Skywork Design enables instant generation of professional-quality images through text prompts or image editing.
actual_capabilities: Generate new images from text descriptions using Skywork AI API
Edit existing images with text instructions (style transfer, element addition/removal)
Use multiple reference images for consistent image editing
Control output resolution (1K, 2K, 4K) and aspect ratio (9 options)
Create specialized content with built-in scenario workflows (logos, branding, social media, posters)
Access professional design guidance through integrated scenario files
limitations: Requires valid Skywork AI account and authentication
Edit mode supports up to 4 characters and 10 objects per workflow

use_cases.title: Marketing teams create visual assets on demand
use_cases.description: Generate social media graphics, promotional banners, and marketing materials without design software. Just describe what you need.

use_cases.title: Design teams produce brand consistency
use_cases.description: Create logos, brand guidelines, and visual identity materials using built-in branding workflows that maintain visual consistency.

use_cases.title: Content creators generate thumbnails and covers
use_cases.description: Quickly produce YouTube thumbnails, podcast covers, and blog imagery with platform-specific aspect ratios and aesthetics.

prompt_templates.title: Simple image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [type of image] with [main subject], [style], [colors], and [mood].

prompt_templates.title: Detailed scene creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image showing [detailed scene description] with [specific lighting], [camera angle], [style reference]. The image should convey [emotion/message].

prompt_templates.title: Image editing with reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the provided image by [specific change 1], [specific change 2]. Maintain the [elements to preserve]. Style should be [desired style].

prompt_templates.title: Brand-consistent series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [type of content] for [brand/project name] following the visual style from [reference image]. Use [brand colors], [brand mood]. [Additional specific requirements].

output_examples.input: Generate a minimalist tech startup logo with blue gradient and geometric shapes
output_examples.output: A professional logo design featuring a geometric icon with blue gradient coloring, suitable for tech industry branding

output_examples.input: Edit this product photo to have a warmer lighting and remove the background
output_examples.output: The product image with warm golden lighting and transparent background, ready for e-commerce use

output_examples.input: Create an Instagram story graphic promoting a summer sale with tropical theme
output_examples.output: A vibrant 9:16 story graphic with tropical elements, bold typography announcing the sale, and summer color palette

best_practices: Provide detailed, descriptive prompts with specific visual elements, style references, and mood descriptions rather than just keywords
Use the built-in scenario files for specialized content types to get optimized prompts and workflows for logos, branding, social media, and other formats
Maintain consistency across multiple images by using the output of one generation as input-image for subsequent edits
anti_patterns: Using vague prompts like 'make it look good' - specificity drives quality; always include concrete visual details and style references
Skipping authentication verification before starting image generation - always run the auth script first to ensure valid token
Generating at maximum resolution (4K) for initial drafts - start at 1K or 2K for faster iteration, upgrade to 4K only for final deliverables

faq.question: What authentication is required?
faq.answer: Skywork account authentication via browser login or SKYBOT_TOKEN environment variable. Run the auth script once to cache your token.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: Any standard image format (PNG, JPG, WEBP) can be used as input for editing. Output is always PNG format.

faq.question: How long does image generation take?
faq.answer: Generation typically takes 30-120 seconds depending on complexity and server load. The script shows progress updates during generation.

faq.question: What resolution should I use?
faq.answer: Use 1K for quick drafts, 2K for most deliverables, and 4K only for final high-resolution output to save time and resources.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use multiple -i flags to provide multiple reference images. The edit mode supports up to 4 characters and 10 objects in a single workflow.

faq.question: Where are generated images saved?
faq.answer: Images save to your current working directory. The script outputs both the local file path and an OSS URL for cross-platform access.
